description: Phase 3 — Digest Provenance v2: Add derived_from_ids metadata field, manager-level provenance indexes, lithos_provenance MCP tool, and surface lineage in all read/search responses

userStories.title: Introduce structured WriteResult type
userStories.description: As a developer, I need manager methods to return a structured result type instead of KnowledgeDocument | dict unions, so the server layer can extract warnings, status, and document data without fragile isinstance checks.
acceptanceCriteria: Define a WriteResult dataclass in knowledge.py with fields: status: Literal['created', 'updated', 'duplicate', 'error'], document: KnowledgeDocument | None, warnings: list[str], error_code: str | None, message: str | None, and duplicate_of: DuplicateInfo | None
Define DuplicateInfo as a TypedDict or dataclass with id: str, title: str, source_url: str | None
create() returns WriteResult instead of KnowledgeDocument | dict
update() returns WriteResult instead of KnowledgeDocument | dict
Server layer (server.py) maps WriteResult fields to the MCP response envelope — no more isinstance dispatch on dict vs document
Existing tests updated to use WriteResult accessors
Pyright type check passes
Ruff lint and format pass
All existing tests pass (uv run --extra dev pytest -m 'not integration' tests/ -q)
userStories.notes: This is foundational — all subsequent stories depend on WriteResult. Follow the existing return patterns in create() and update() in knowledge.py. The server.py tool handlers currently do isinstance checks on dict vs KnowledgeDocument; replace with WriteResult attribute access. See src/lithos/knowledge.py lines 310-322 for existing _UnsetType pattern as a model for singleton/sentinel design.

userStories.title: Add derived_from_ids to KnowledgeMetadata
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to store declared lineage in frontmatter so it persists on disk and survives read-write cycles.
acceptanceCriteria: KnowledgeMetadata has field derived_from_ids: list[str] defaulting to []
to_dict() serializes derived_from_ids only when non-empty (omit empty list from YAML)
from_dict() deserializes derived_from_ids from frontmatter, defaulting to [] when absent
derived_from_ids added to _KNOWN_METADATA_KEYS set
Round-trip test: write a doc with derived_from_ids, read it back, values match
Round-trip test: existing doc without derived_from_ids reads back as []
Pyright type check passes
Ruff lint and format pass
All existing tests pass
userStories.notes: Follow the source_url pattern in KnowledgeMetadata.to_dict() / from_dict() — conditional serialization when non-empty. See knowledge.py lines 128-154 (to_dict) and 156-191 (from_dict). The field is list[str] not str|None, so serialization condition is 'if self.derived_from_ids' (truthy non-empty list).

userStories.title: Add provenance validation and normalization helpers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need validation logic that rejects invalid derived_from_ids entries and normalizes valid ones, so invariants are enforced at the manager layer.
acceptanceCriteria: Add validate_derived_from_ids(ids: list[str], self_id: str | None = None) function in knowledge.py that returns normalized list or raises ValueError
Validation rejects non-UUID strings (titles, slugs, paths, empty strings, whitespace)
Validation rejects malformed UUIDs (wrong format, wrong length)
Normalization trims whitespace, lowercases, deduplicates, and sorts the list
Uppercase UUIDs are normalized to lowercase (not rejected)
Self-reference detection: if self_id is provided and appears in the list, raise ValueError
Valid UUIDs (any UUID version, lowercase hex with hyphens) are accepted
Unit tests cover: valid UUIDs, uppercase UUIDs normalized to lowercase, malformed UUIDs, non-UUID strings, self-reference, empty list, duplicates, whitespace trimming, sort order
Pyright type check passes
Ruff lint and format pass
userStories.notes: Follow the normalize_url() pattern (knowledge.py lines 61-107) as a model for standalone validation function. Use uuid.UUID() constructor for validation — catch ValueError. Normalize by: strip whitespace, lowercase, parse with uuid.UUID to validate format, convert back to str, then sorted(set(results)).

userStories.title: Add manager-level provenance indexes and two-pass startup scan
userStories.description: As a developer, I need in-memory provenance indexes in KnowledgeManager so lineage queries are fast without scanning all documents.
acceptanceCriteria: KnowledgeManager.__init__ initializes _doc_to_sources: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
KnowledgeManager.__init__ initializes _source_to_derived: dict[str, set[str]] = {}
KnowledgeManager.__init__ initializes _unresolved_provenance: dict[str, set[str]] = {}
KnowledgeManager.__init__ initializes _id_to_title: dict[str, str] = {}
_scan_existing() clears all four new indexes before rebuilding (prevents stale accumulation on repeated scans)

_scan_existing() uses a two-pass approach: pass 1 walks files and populates _id_to_path, _slug_to_id, _source_url_to_id, _id_to_title, and collects each doc's derived_from_ids into a temporary list; pass 2 iterates collected provenance pairs and classifies each source ID as resolved (exists in _id_to_path) or unresolved

Unit test: after scanning a directory with provenance relationships, all three provenance indexes are correct — including forward references (doc A references doc B, but B's file sorts alphabetically after A)
Unit test: unresolved references are correctly identified only for genuinely missing IDs, not for scan-order artifacts
Unit test: repeated _scan_existing() calls produce identical index state
Pyright type check passes
Ruff lint and format pass
userStories.notes: The key insight is the two-pass scan. Current _scan_existing() (knowledge.py lines 339-390) does a single walk populating _id_to_path etc. Refactor to: (1) keep existing pass 1 logic but also read derived_from_ids from each doc's metadata into a temp list like deferred_provenance: list[tuple[str, list[str]]] = []; (2) after the walk completes, iterate deferred_provenance and for each (doc_id, source_ids) pair, classify each source_id by checking _id_to_path.

userStories.title: Maintain provenance indexes on create
userStories.description: As a developer, I need create() to update provenance indexes so newly created documents and their lineage are immediately queryable.
acceptanceCriteria: create() accepts derived_from_ids: list[str] | None = None parameter
On create with None or omitted: store [] in metadata, set _doc_to_sources[doc_id] = []
On create with non-empty list: validate via validate_derived_from_ids(), normalize, store in metadata; update _doc_to_sources and _source_to_derived; put unresolved refs in _unresolved_provenance
On create with invalid entries (non-UUID): return WriteResult with status='error', error_code='invalid_input' — do not create the document

After creating a doc, check _unresolved_provenance for any existing documents that reference the new doc's ID; move those entries from _unresolved_provenance to _source_to_derived (no frontmatter mutation)
Update _id_to_title with the new doc's title
Warnings list includes 'derived_from_ids contains missing document: <uuid>' for each unresolved source ID
create() returns WriteResult that includes warnings
Unit tests: create with no provenance, create with valid provenance, create with unresolved refs, create resolving previously-unresolved refs, create with invalid UUIDs
Pyright type check passes
userStories.notes: The auto-resolve path (checking _unresolved_provenance after create) is primarily exercised by file-watcher ingestion or startup scan of externally-created files (e.g., git-restored notes with their original UUID), not by normal MCP lithos_write create which always mints a fresh UUID. Still must be implemented and tested.

userStories.title: Maintain provenance indexes on update
userStories.description: As a developer, I need update() to handle omit-vs-clear-vs-replace semantics for derived_from_ids following the unified write contract.
acceptanceCriteria: update() accepts derived_from_ids: list[str] | None | _UnsetType = _UNSET parameter
_UNSET (omitted): preserve existing derived_from_ids — no index changes
[] (empty list via None): clear existing provenance — remove from all provenance indexes, store [] in metadata
Non-empty list: validate via validate_derived_from_ids(ids, self_id=doc_id), normalize, replace entire set — remove old index entries, add new ones
Self-reference rejected: if the document's own ID is in the new derived_from_ids, return WriteResult with status='error', error_code='invalid_input'
Invalid UUIDs rejected with WriteResult status='error', error_code='invalid_input'
Update _id_to_title if title changed
Warnings returned for unresolved source IDs
Unit tests: omit (preserve), clear ([]), replace with valid IDs, replace with unresolved IDs, self-reference rejection, invalid UUID rejection
Conformance test: _UNSET, []/None, and non-empty list are distinguishable for derived_from_ids
Pyright type check passes
userStories.notes: Follow the source_url omit-vs-clear pattern in update() (knowledge.py lines 550-651). For derived_from_ids the semantics differ slightly: _UNSET=preserve, []=clear (not None=clear like source_url), non-empty list=replace. The MCP boundary mapping (US-008) will handle converting None->_UNSET.

userStories.title: Maintain provenance indexes on delete
userStories.description: As a developer, I need delete() to clean up provenance indexes without mutating other documents' frontmatter.
acceptanceCriteria: When a document is deleted, remove its entry from _doc_to_sources
Remove the deleted doc's ID from all _source_to_derived values where it appears as a derived doc
For documents that declared the deleted doc as a source: move those relationships from _source_to_derived[deleted_id] to _unresolved_provenance[deleted_id]
Remove the deleted doc from _id_to_title
Do NOT mutate any other document's frontmatter
Unit test: delete a source doc, verify derived docs now show unresolved provenance in _unresolved_provenance
Unit test: delete a derived doc, verify source doc's _source_to_derived entry is cleaned up
Pyright type check passes
userStories.notes: delete() is at knowledge.py lines 653-689. It currently removes from _id_to_path, _slug_to_id, _source_url_to_id. Add provenance cleanup in the same method. Key: when deleting doc X, if _source_to_derived[X] has entries (docs derived FROM X), those relationships become unresolved. Also remove X from _source_to_derived[s] for each s in _doc_to_sources[X].

userStories.title: Wire derived_from_ids into lithos_write MCP tool
userStories.description: As an agent, I want to pass derived_from_ids to lithos_write so I can declare lineage when creating or updating notes.
acceptanceCriteria: lithos_write tool signature includes derived_from_ids: list[str] | None = None
On create: pass derived_from_ids to manager.create()
On update: map MCP boundary semantics to manager semantics: None (omitted) -> _UNSET (preserve); [] -> [] (clear); non-empty list -> pass through (replace)
Warnings from the WriteResult are included in the response envelope warnings field
Integration test: create via tool with derived_from_ids, read back, values match
Integration test: update via tool preserving (None), clearing ([]), and replacing derived_from_ids
Pyright type check passes
All tests pass (uv run --extra dev pytest tests/ -q)
userStories.notes: Follow the source_url MCP boundary mapping pattern in server.py lines 250-280. For derived_from_ids: None at MCP boundary means omitted (preserve on update); [] means clear; non-empty list means replace. The create path is simpler — just pass through (None becomes [] in manager).

userStories.title: Add manager-owned sync_from_disk API for external file changes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need KnowledgeManager to own provenance index maintenance for external file changes, so the server/watcher layer does not mutate manager-owned state indirectly.
acceptanceCriteria: KnowledgeManager exposes sync_from_disk(path: Path) -> KnowledgeDocument that re-reads a file from disk, updates all manager indexes (_id_to_path, _slug_to_id, _source_url_to_id, _id_to_title, and all three provenance indexes), and returns the parsed document for downstream search/graph indexing

sync_from_disk handles both new files (create) and modified files (update) uniformly: read the file, diff against current index state, apply changes — the current watcher sends deleted=False for both cases with no create vs modify distinction
sync_from_disk for a new file checks _unresolved_provenance for auto-resolution
sync_from_disk for a modified file diffs derived_from_ids against _doc_to_sources, removes stale entries, adds new ones, reclassifies resolved/unresolved
sync_from_disk updates _id_to_title (handles external title changes)
handle_file_change in server.py calls sync_from_disk for non-delete events instead of directly mutating manager state
handle_file_change in server.py delegates delete to manager.delete() which already handles provenance cleanup (US-007)
File watcher provenance errors are caught and logged, never crash the watcher
Integration test: externally create a file with derived_from_ids, verify provenance indexes update
Integration test: externally modify derived_from_ids in a file, verify indexes update
Integration test: externally change a file's title, verify _id_to_title updates
Integration test: externally delete a file that is a provenance source, verify indexes mark relationships as unresolved
Pyright type check passes
userStories.notes: Current handle_file_change (server.py lines 160-203) directly calls knowledge.read() and search.index_document() etc. Refactor the non-delete path to call knowledge.sync_from_disk(path) which returns the doc, then pass that doc to search/graph. The delete path already calls knowledge.delete(). This follows the 'manager owns invariants' rule from digest-auto-link-v2.md design principles.

userStories.title: Add _rebuild_indices provenance support
userStories.description: As a developer, I need _rebuild_indices() to include provenance index rebuilding so full rebuilds leave provenance consistent.
acceptanceCriteria: _rebuild_indices() in server.py clears and rebuilds provenance indexes as part of the full rebuild path
This can be achieved by calling _scan_existing() which already clears and rebuilds provenance indexes (US-004), or by an explicit provenance rebuild step
Integration test: after _rebuild_indices(), provenance indexes match on-disk state
Pyright type check passes
userStories.notes: See server.py lines 108-155 for _rebuild_indices(). It currently iterates docs and calls search.index_document() and graph.add_document(). Since _scan_existing() already builds provenance indexes (US-004), and _rebuild_indices() calls knowledge scan paths, this may already work. Verify and add explicit test.

userStories.title: Add lithos_provenance MCP tool — core BFS traversal
userStories.description: As an agent, I want to query document lineage so I can understand what a digest was derived from or what documents cite a given source.
acceptanceCriteria: New MCP tool lithos_provenance registered with signature: lithos_provenance(id: str, direction: str = 'both', depth: int = 1, include_unresolved: bool = True) -> dict
direction='sources': returns documents this doc is derived from
direction='derived': returns documents derived from this doc
direction='both': returns both sources and derived
Each node in results is { id, title } with title resolved from _id_to_title cache — no per-node file I/O
depth is clamped to range 1-3
BFS traversal over provenance indexes only (NOT wiki-link graph), tracking visited nodes to handle cycles
sources, derived, and unresolved_sources lists are sorted by ID for deterministic output
When include_unresolved=True, response includes unresolved_sources list of UUID strings
When include_unresolved=False, unresolved sources are omitted
If id does not exist in _id_to_path, return error: {status: 'error', code: 'doc_not_found', message: '...'}
Response shape: { id, sources: [{id, title}], derived: [{id, title}], unresolved_sources: [uuid_str] }
Integration test: single-depth sources query
Integration test: single-depth derived query
Integration test: multi-depth BFS (depth=2) with chain A->B->C
Integration test: cycle handling (A derives from B, B derives from A)
Integration test: unresolved sources included/excluded
Integration test: unknown ID returns doc_not_found
Pyright type check passes
userStories.notes: Register via @self.mcp.tool() inside _register_tools(). BFS implementation: use collections.deque for the frontier, set for visited. For direction='sources' walk _doc_to_sources; for 'derived' walk _source_to_derived. At each depth level, expand all current-level nodes before incrementing depth. Clamp depth with min(max(depth, 1), 3). Sort final lists by node ID.

userStories.title: Surface derived_from_ids in read/search/list responses
userStories.description: As an agent, I want to see derived_from_ids in read, search, semantic, and list responses so I can discover lineage without a separate provenance call.
acceptanceCriteria: lithos_read response metadata dict includes derived_from_ids (list, possibly empty)
lithos_search result items include derived_from_ids field
lithos_semantic result items include derived_from_ids field
lithos_list result items include derived_from_ids field
For lithos_read: derived_from_ids comes from the already-loaded document metadata (no extra I/O)
For lithos_search and lithos_semantic: derived_from_ids is resolved from the manager's _doc_to_sources in-memory index (keyed by doc ID from search results), not from per-result file reads
For lithos_list: derived_from_ids comes from already-loaded document metadata
Integration test: create doc with provenance, verify lithos_read includes it
Integration test: create doc with provenance, verify it appears in lithos_search results with derived_from_ids
Pyright type check passes
userStories.notes: For lithos_read (server.py ~line 362): add meta['derived_from_ids'] = doc.metadata.derived_from_ids similar to how source_url is added. For lithos_search/semantic (server.py ~lines 455-523): look up self.knowledge._doc_to_sources.get(r.id, []) for each result. For lithos_list (server.py ~lines 568-581): use d.metadata.derived_from_ids directly.

userStories.title: OTEL instrumentation for provenance paths
userStories.description: As an operator, I need provenance operations to emit spans and metrics via the existing telemetry foundation, per the architecture guardrails.
acceptanceCriteria: lithos_provenance tool handler emits a span consistent with existing tool handlers (manual tracer spans in server.py)
Provenance validation/normalization in write paths is covered by the existing write span (provenance warnings recorded as span events or attributes)
Provenance index rebuild during _scan_existing() emits a log with count of resolved/unresolved references
No raw content or full ID lists in span attributes (per guardrails sensitive-data policy) — use counts instead
Pyright type check passes
userStories.notes: Follow existing tool handler span pattern in server.py — e.g., tracer = get_tracer(); with tracer.start_as_current_span('lithos.provenance') as span. For scan stats, a logger.info with resolved/unresolved counts is sufficient.

userStories.title: Conformance test suite for provenance
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a conformance test suite proving provenance behavior aligns with the unified write contract and architecture guardrails.
acceptanceCriteria: Conformance test: create with derived_from_ids=None stores []
Conformance test: create with valid UUIDs stores normalized list
Conformance test: update with _UNSET preserves existing provenance
Conformance test: update with [] clears provenance
Conformance test: update with new list replaces provenance
Conformance test: self-reference on update is rejected with invalid_input
Conformance test: malformed UUIDs rejected with invalid_input
Conformance test: non-UUID identifiers (titles, slugs, paths) rejected
Conformance test: uppercase UUIDs normalized to lowercase
Conformance test: unresolved source IDs produce warnings, not errors
Conformance test: file-watcher ingestion of a source doc auto-resolves previously-unresolved provenance
Conformance test: deleting a source doc marks provenance as unresolved
Conformance test: provenance cycles don't cause infinite traversal
Conformance test: single-write contract parity (create/update/duplicate/warnings envelope)
Conformance test: two-pass scan correctly resolves forward references at startup
Conformance test: repeated _scan_existing() calls produce identical index state
Conformance test: external title change via file watcher updates _id_to_title
All tests pass in CI (unit + integration + lint + format + type check)
Pyright type check passes
userStories.notes: Create tests/test_provenance_conformance.py. This is the cross-cutting conformance suite from final-architecture-guardrails.md section 7. Tests should exercise both manager-level and MCP-tool-level paths. Use the existing test_config fixture for temp directories.

userStories.title: Update docs/SPECIFICATION.md for Phase 3
userStories.description: As a user of the Lithos specification, I need the spec to document derived_from_ids and lithos_provenance so the spec matches the implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: Section 3.2 (Knowledge File Format): derived_from_ids listed in frontmatter example with description
Section 5.1 (lithos_write): derived_from_ids parameter description matches implementation
New section within or after Graph Operations: lithos_provenance tool fully documented (arguments table, returns, behavior notes for BFS/depth/cycles/unresolved)
Tool count in Appendix B updated (20 -> 21)
Appendix B tool summary table updated to include lithos_provenance under Graph or Provenance category
lithos_read return type documents derived_from_ids in metadata
lithos_search, lithos_semantic, lithos_list return types document derived_from_ids field
No contradictions with unified-write-contract.md or final-architecture-guardrails.md
Pyright type check passes
Ruff lint and format pass
userStories.notes: docs/SPECIFICATION.md already lists derived_from_ids on lithos_write (line 271) but the code didn't support it until this phase. Verify the existing spec text matches the implementation, then add lithos_provenance as a new tool section and update response shapes for read/search/semantic/list.
